topic: Someone learns their favorite author plagiarized their stories
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara reread the manuscript under the harsh glare of her desk lamp. The words, her intricate tapestry of character arcs and world-building, were undeniably there, but twisted, hollowed out. A cold knot tightened in her stomach, spreading outward until her hands began to tremble slightly. She traced a passage, a sequence of dialogue she’d poured weeks of sleepless nights into crafting, and saw the unmistakable echo of another writer’s clumsy phrasing. "This is impossible," she whispered, the sound brittle in the quiet study. She slammed the document shut, the sharp crack echoing the sudden fury building behind her eyes. She stood up, pacing the length of her room, her movements jerky and agitated. Every corner of the room seemed to hold the ghost of the copier, the careless hand that had stolen her voice.

[story 2]

"You can't be serious," Marcus said, his voice dangerously low as he stared at the comparison. He leaned back in his chair, the leather groaning beneath him, and ran a hand roughly through his already disheveled hair. The realization hit him like a physical blow—the core of his celebrated novel, the very soul he’d labored over, was merely a thinly veiled imitation of a lesser-known contemporary. A flush crept up his neck, hot and undeniable. He snatched the printed comparison, crumpling the page with unnecessary force. "This is an insult," he muttered, his jaw tight enough to crack. He began methodically gathering his notes, his movements sharp and decisive, a silent vow forming in his gaze that he would expose this trespass, no matter the cost.

[story 3]

I felt the blood rush to my face, a sudden, unwelcome heat blooming across my cheeks. When I finally opened the draft, the familiar cadence of my own prose was undermined by jarringly familiar structures, borrowed phrases that felt like stolen jewels. My breath hitched. I dropped the pen; it clattered onto the wooden floor, the sound disproportionately loud in the silence. My fists clenched so tightly that my knuckles turned white. I looked around the room, suddenly seeing every object as a potential witness to this profound violation. A deep, shaky exhale escaped me, followed by a slow, deliberate straightening of my posture. The tremor in my hands subsided, replaced by a hard, unwavering focus. I knew exactly what needed to happen next.
